title: Causal Trajectory Summaries Within Each Stint
summary: Current best features are mostly single-row heuristics.
rationale: Current best features are mostly single-row heuristics. Dynamic prediction literature and recent F1 sequence models both emphasize that event risk improves when you summarize the recent trajectory, not just the latest value.
implementation_hint: Within each sorted `(Race, Year, Driver, Stint)` sequence, add trailing 3/5-lap causal features for `LapTime (s)`, `LapTime_Delta`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, `Position_Change`: last value, EWMA, slope, acceleration, std, max deterioration, and a shock flag when the latest point deviates sharply from the recent window. Keep windows strictly backward-looking.
expected_effect: Better detection of tyre-cliff, traffic, and pre-stop buildup patterns that current row-wise features miss.
risk: Easy to leak if windows are centered or sorting is wrong; early-stint windows will be sparse and noisy.
